decks.notes: The deck focuses on using aggressive creatures and disruptive spells to control the board while steadily building up a strong presence. It utilizes Aether Vial to quickly deploy creatures, along with efficient removal like Swords to Plowshares and Thoughtseize to handle threats. The deck also includes cards like Karakas and Skyclave Apparition to deal with problematic creatures, while Stoneforge Mystic fetches powerful equipment to boost its creatures. Overall, the deck aims to pressure opponents with its aggressive strategy while disrupting their game plan to secure victory.

The main focus of this Boros Aggro deck is to quickly flood the board with aggressive creatures like Agate Instigator, Amped Raptor, and Guide of Souls, supported by efficient removal spells such as Swords to Plowshares. The deck also aims to disrupt opponents with cards like Static Prison and Hexing Squelcher while gaining value from cards like Goblin Bombardment and Ajani, Nacatl Pariah. Additionally, the mana base includes fetch lands like Arid Mesa and Marsh Flats to ensure consistent mana fixing and to trigger landfall abilities. The overall strategy is to overwhelm opponents with early aggression and efficient disruption.

The main concept of this deck is to lock down the opponent's actions and resources using a combination of artifacts like Trinisphere, Karn, the Great Creator, and Planar Nexus. The deck aims to control the game by limiting the opponent's options and disrupting their strategy with cards like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Kozilek's Command. The inclusion of powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and The One Ring provide additional utility and advantage. The deck is designed to establish a strong board presence and control the game until it can win with powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn.

The main concept of this deck is to use a combination of efficient spells and tempo plays to control the game and put pressure on the opponent. With a focus on cheap instant and sorcery spells, as well as disruptive elements like Daze and Force of Will, the deck aims to maintain board presence with threats like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent while disrupting the opponent's game plan. The deck also utilizes cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder to dig for key cards and keep up with the opponent in card advantage.

This Doomsday combo deck focuses on assembling the necessary cards to cast Doomsday and win the game with Thassa's Oracle. Key cards include Brainstorm, Dark Ritual, Force of Will, Lotus Petal, and Underground Sea to efficiently reach the combo. The deck also includes control elements like Daze, Thoughtseize, and Swords to Plowshares to disrupt opponents' game plans. The land base is optimized for consistency and includes both fetch lands and dual lands for mana fixing. The goal is to quickly and efficiently execute the win condition while maintaining a level of control over the game.

The deck focuses on utilizing lands to control the game, with key cards like Dark Depths and Thespian's Stage for a quick Merit Lage token, along with Life from the Loam to recur lands. It also disrupts opponents with cards like Sphere of Resistance and Wasteland, while Boseiju, Who Endures protects important spells like Crop Rotation. The deck aims to lock opponents out of the game by controlling the board and resources, while slowly building towards a win condition with powerful land synergies.

The deck focuses on creating a lockdown through a combination of artifact cards that hinder the opponent's ability to play spells or activate abilities, while also utilizing powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storm to control the board and generate advantage. The deck also has a win condition with Emrakul, the Aeons Torn, using cards like The One Ring and Planar Nexus to cheat it into play and close out the game. Overall, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy and secure victory through powerful artifact synergies.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly and aggressively control the game tempo by using powerful UR spells and creatures. The deck focuses on disrupting the opponent with counterspells like Force of Will and Daze, while also maintaining pressure with cards like Delver of Secrets and Murktide Regent. By combining efficient removal spells like Lightning Bolt and Chain Lightning with cantrips like Brainstorm and Ponder, the deck aims to maintain card advantage and consistently apply pressure on the opponent. The mana base is optimized for consistency and to support the deck's aggressive playstyle.

This deck focuses on utilizing Gaea's Cradle and mana dorks to generate massive amounts of mana to ramp into powerful threats like Craterhoof Behemoth and Atraxa, Grand Unifier. The deck also has control elements such as Wasteland and Endurance to disrupt the opponent's strategy while maintaining a strong board presence. The combination of mana acceleration, powerful creatures, and disruptive spells allows the deck to control the game and overwhelm the opponent with its powerful threats.

The deck focuses on ramping up mana quickly with cards like Gaea's Cradle and Birds of Paradise to cast powerful creatures like Craterhoof Behemoth and Grist, the Hunger Tide. It also includes disruption strategies with cards like Thoughtseize and Collector Ouphe to control the opponent's plays. The deck aims to establish board control and overwhelm the opponent with powerful threats while disrupting their game plan. It also incorporates toolbox creatures like Allosaurus Shepherd and Endurance to deal with specific threats effectively.

The main concept of this deck is to efficiently control the board through disruptive spells like Brainstorm, Daze, Fatal Push, Force of Will, and Thoughtseize while also maintaining tempo with aggressive creatures like Murktide Regent and Orcish Bowmasters. The deck is designed to continually disrupt the opponent's game plan while also applying pressure with its creatures. It also utilizes card advantage engines like Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student and can control the opponent's mana base with Wasteland. Overall, the deck aims to maintain control of the game while applying aggressive pressure to secure victory.

The deck focuses on using Dark Ritual and Doomsday to set up a winning combination with cards like Thassa's Oracle and Flow State. It includes counterspells like Force of Will and Daze for protection, along with card draw and manipulation with Brainstorm and Ponder. Murktide Regent provides a secondary win condition, while Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student helps dig for key pieces. The mana base includes efficient lands like Underground Sea and Polluted Delta, with some disruption from Wasteland. The goal is to assemble the combo quickly and protect it until victory.

The main concept of this deck is to use powerful Eldrazi creatures to quickly overwhelm the opponent in an aggressive manner. By utilizing cards like Chalice of the Void to disrupt the opponent's strategy and Eldrazi Temple to ramp up mana, the deck aims to play big threats like Reality Smasher and Thought-Knot Seer ahead of schedule. The inclusion of cards like Glaring Fleshraker and It That Heralds the End further supports the aggro strategy by providing additional pressure on the opponent. This deck seeks to win the game quickly through aggressive creature plays and disruption.

The main concept of this Eldrazi Aggro deck is to overwhelm the opponent with powerful Eldrazi creatures and disrupt their game plan with cards like Chalice of the Void and Thought-Knot Seer. The deck aims to quickly deploy threats using fast mana sources like Ancient Tomb and Lotus Petal, while also utilizing cards like Eldrazi Confluence and Kozilek's Command to control the board and finish off the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures with disruptive elements, the deck seeks to apply pressure and close out games efficiently.

The deck utilizes artifacts to create a lock on the game, preventing opponents from effectively playing their cards. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Trinisphere, and Pithing Needle, the deck is able to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. With powerful mana acceleration from cards like Urza's Tower and Grim Monolith, the deck can quickly establish a dominant board presence. Emrakul, the Aeons Torn serves as a win condition to close out the game once the opponent's resources have been depleted.

The main goal of this deck is to maintain control of the game by playing aggressively with efficient creatures like Dragon's Rage Channeler and Murktide Regent while disrupting the opponent's strategy with counterspells like Daze and Force of Will. The deck also aims to dig through the library for essential cards with cantrips like Brainstorm, Ponder, and Preordain, while also utilizing fetch lands like Flooded Strand and Scalding Tarn to thin the deck and fix mana. Wasteland is included to disrupt the opponent's mana base, allowing this deck to maintain tempo and pressure throughout the game.
